pairs.question: Where and when was Devon Malcolm Endersby born?
pairs.answer: Devon Malcolm Endersby was born on 12 May 1992 in East London, Eastern Cape, South Africa.

pairs.question: What educational institutions did Devon Malcolm Endersby attend in England?
pairs.answer: After moving to England, Devon Malcolm Endersby attended Tanbridge House School and The College of Richard Collyer in Horsham, followed by Loughborough University.

pairs.question: Describe Devon Malcolm Endersby's first-class cricket career at Loughborough MCCU.
pairs.answer: Endersby played first-class cricket for Loughborough MCCU from 2012 to 2014, making six appearances. He scored 107 runs with an average of 11.88 and a high score of 53. As a right-arm medium-fast bowler, he took 4 wickets from 69 overs bowled.

pairs.question: In which year did Devon Malcolm Endersby make his List A one-day debut, and against which team?
pairs.answer: Endersby made his List A one-day debut in 2013 against Gloucestershire at Bristol during the Yorkshire Bank 40 tournament.

pairs.question: How many List A appearances did Devon Malcolm Endersby make in 2013, and how many wickets did he take?
pairs.answer: In 2013, Endersby made six List A appearances and took one wicket from 26 overs bowled. His only wicket was the Ireland international Paul Stirling, who was playing for Middlesex.

pairs.question: Where does Devon Malcolm Endersby currently live?
pairs.answer: Devon Malcolm Endersby currently resides in Australia.

pairs.question: What role did Devon Malcolm Endersby primarily play in cricket, and what were his performance statistics as a bowler?
pairs.answer: Endersby primarily played as a right-arm medium-fast bowler. In first-class cricket, he took 4 wickets from 69 overs bowled, while in List A cricket, he took 1 wicket from 26 overs bowled.
